strengths: Hands-on ETL and pipeline building with Python
Quantified impact on reporting and operations
weaknesses: Limited tenure in dedicated data engineering roles
Unclear experience connecting BI tools directly to BigQuery
improvement_bullets: Integrated Salesforce CRM data with PostgreSQL databases and utilized dbt to build clean, analytics-ready tables for downstream reporting, reducing data reconciliation time by 30%.
Architected and maintained data pipelines in a fast-paced startup environment, transitioning manual workflows into automated, scalable processes within our cloud data warehouse.
Engineered modular data transformation layers using dbt and SQL to ensure high data quality and consistency across all reporting environments.
Connected Power BI directly to BigQuery to automate live dashboard reporting, ensuring stakeholders had access to real-time, accurate business metrics.
Built and maintained robust, analytics-ready tables to serve cross-functional teams, ensuring data reliability and speed for critical business decision-making.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Analytics Engineer opportunity with Pivotal Health. I am interested in this opportunity because of my deep technical foundation in data engineering and my passion for making complex datasets actionable in high-growth environments. What interests me about working for Pivotal Health is your mission to bring fairness and clarity to healthcare reimbursement through integrated, AI-driven solutions; I am inspired by the way you bridge the gap between technical complexity and real-world provider impact. I believe my expertise in building automated data pipelines and my experience in data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on business-centric outcomes. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our reporting workflow and designed an automated data pipeline using Python and Power BI; this initiative improved KPI visibility and reduced manual processing time by 40%. Ultimately, I know that the Analytics Engineer role at Pivotal Health would allow me to bring my technical skills to the mission-critical task of optimizing your reimbursement data infrastructure. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my skills in cross-departmental collaboration. In this role, I served as the bridge between technical execution and business strategy, translating operational needs into streamlined data workflows. This led to a 300% increase in new customer acquisition by identifying high-value segments through predictive modeling. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my SQL, Python, and dbt skills in working on building the next generation of your analytics warehouse at Pivotal Health. I know that my proficiency in modern cloud data warehouses and my ability to own the end-to-end ETL process align with what it takes to succeed at the Analytics Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
